title: Promptorium parvulorum
summary: The repository XML is a copy of the University of Michigan CME digital text, manually keyed and reviewed from selected portions of Albert Way's three-part 1843-1865 Camden Society edition. Way used British Library Harley MS 221 as his base text and collated five other manuscript sources plus the Pynson 1499, Julian Notary 1508, and Wynkyn de Worde 1516 printings. This record keeps the abstract medieval work, its expanding manuscript redactions, physical witnesses, early print versions, Way's editorial edition, U-M's encoding, the repository artifact, print scans, and the two printed manuscript plates distinct.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00028
entities.description: The checked-in XML artifact, not the medieval work, a manuscript, or the Way print volume.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME00028
entities.description: Public HTML/SGML-derived text keyed from selected portions of Way's edition during the 2016-2018 CME expansion.

entities.label: Albert Way, Promptorium Parvulorum sive Clericorum (1843-1865)
entities.description: Three-part Camden Society edition and commentary, issued as publications 25, 54, and 89 and used as the immediate source of the CME encoding.

bibliographic.citation: Albert Way, ed., Promptorium Parvulorum sive Clericorum: Lexicon Anglo-Latinum Princeps, 3 parts, Camden Society publications 25, 54, and 89 (London: Camden Society, 1843-1865).
bibliographic.title: Promptorium Parvulorum sive Clericorum: Lexicon Anglo-Latinum Princeps

entities.label: Internet Archive/Google scan of a complete bound Way 1843-1865 volume
entities.description: A 673-page scan containing all three Camden Society parts and their supporting matter.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete PDF, OCR text, and OCR XML

entities.label: Two printed manuscript plates in Way's edition
entities.description: Black-and-white printed plates reproducing Harley MS 221 f. 7v and Add MS 22556 f. 73v. They are isolated page specimens, not whole-manuscript surrogates.
entities.reproduction_format: Two printed lithographic or photographic facsimile plates within the complete Way scan

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 221
entities.description: Way's base manuscript; a fifteenth-century parchment copy of the Promptorium at ff. 1r-205v, with adjacent recipes on f. 206r. The current catalog cautions that the copy itself is probably not East Anglian.

physical_description.extent: 206 folios, plus modern preliminary and terminal leaves
notes: The Promptorium occupies ff. 1r-205v; medical and household recipes follow on f. 206r.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 2274, ff. 63r-77v
entities.description: A disordered fragment of the Promptorium in a composite miscellany; the current component record lists portions of G, H, M, N, O, and R, while Way reports readings from G, H, M, N, and R.

date_statements.label: Promptorium fragment date
date_statements.value: second half of the fifteenth century

physical_description.extent: Promptorium fragment ff. 63r-77v within a 77-folio composite

entities.label: King's College Cambridge, MS 8
entities.description: Late-fifteenth-century parchment Promptorium used throughout Way's collation under siglum K.

date_statements.label: Manuscript date
date_statements.value: latter part of the fifteenth century

holding.notes: The exact item-level online record was not located; shelfmark and description were cross-checked in Way, Mayhew, Considine, and the British Library witness census.

entities.label: Winchester Cathedral, MS 15
entities.description: Late-fifteenth-century paper schoolbook codex used throughout Way's collation under siglum S and later transcribed directly by A. L. Mayhew.

date_statements.label: Manuscript date
date_statements.value: late fifteenth century; ownership inscription dated 1494

holding.notes: The institutional page supplies a curator enquiry route but no exact public item record.

notes: The codex also contains Latin school texts and retains its original leather-covered oak boards according to Way.

entities.label: British Library, Add MS 37789
entities.description: Late-fifteenth-century Norfolk paper codex: an imperfect Promptorium at ff. 1r-83v followed by the distinct Medulla grammaticae at ff. 84r-174v. Way collated the Promptorium under siglum H.

entities.label: British Library, Add MS 22556
entities.description: Imperfect fifteenth-century parchment Promptorium acquired after the first two Way parts; Way's letter and notes now occupy modern ff. 1-3, and the medieval text survives at ff. 5r-110v.

physical_description.extent: Promptorium ff. 5r-110v, preceded by modern Way material at ff. 1-3

entities.label: Richard Pynson, Promptorius puerorum (1499)
entities.description: The first printed version, extensively collated by Way under siglum P; the edition's prologue and separated nomina/verba arrangement represent a version distinct from any one surviving manuscript.

bibliographic.citation: Promptorius puerorum, printed in London by Richard Pynson at the expense of Frederick Egmondt and Petrus post Pascha, 15 May 1499 (GW 10483; ISTC ip01011000).
bibliographic.title: Promptorius puerorum
bibliographic.publisher: Richard Pynson for Frederick Egmondt and Petrus post Pascha

physical_description.extent: 116 unnumbered leaves in the ideal copy, with t4 blank

entities.label: Penn Libraries complete digital facsimile of Inc P-1011 Kislak copy
entities.description: A 238-canvas IIIF object exposing the complete bound Kislak copy, including covers, pastedowns, endleaves, blanks, all surviving printed surfaces a1-t3, and copy-specific annotations. The ideal blank t4 is absent from this physical copy.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and JPEG page images

entities.label: Julian Notary, Promptorium parvulorum clericorum (1508)
entities.description: Portable quarto printed on 12 August 1508, apparently following de Worde's text; Way selected a few readings under siglum J from the Grenville copy then in the British Museum.

bibliographic.citation: Promptorium paruulorum clericorum, printed in London by Julian Notary, 12 August 1508 (USTC 501001; ESTC S112953).
bibliographic.title: Promptorium paruulorum clericorum

entities.label: Wynkyn de Worde, Promptuarium parvulorum clericorum (1516)
entities.description: Abridged early print version dated 5 September 1516; Way had Sir Frederic Madden's former Heber copy throughout preparation and selected a few readings under siglum W.

bibliographic.citation: Promptuarium parvulorum clericorum, printed in London by Wynkyn de Worde, 5 September 1516 (ESTC S122060).
bibliographic.title: Promptuarium parvulorum clericorum

entities.label: A. L. Mayhew, The Promptorium Parvulorum (1908)
entities.description: Early English Text Society edition directly from Winchester Cathedral MS 15, retaining the manuscript's separate nomina/verba ordering and much grammatical information omitted by Way. It is a later comparison edition, not an upstream source of CME00028.

bibliographic.citation: A. L. Mayhew, ed., The Promptorium Parvulorum: The First English-Latin Dictionary, Early English Text Society Extra Series 102 (London: Oxford University Press, 1908).
bibliographic.title: The Promptorium Parvulorum: The First English-Latin Dictionary
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society Extra Series 102
bibliographic.pages: xxviii and 811 columns, with notes and glossaries

entities.label: Internet Archive/Google scan of Mayhew's 1908 Promptorium
entities.description: Complete 591-page scan of the EETS edition, cached for comparison but not represented as a source of the corpus XML.

locators: Way unnumbered addenda opening and pp. 561-563

object.description: Complete bound three-part Way volume, including all supporting matter
Promptorium text in the base manuscript

scope.notes: Way calls Harley MS 221 the groundwork or text of the edition. The manuscript lacks a run of C verbs, which Way supplied chiefly from Winchester MS 15; the edition is therefore not a diplomatic facsimile of Harley 221.

subject.description: Variants explicitly attributed to Harley MS 2274

subject.description: Variant readings and additions throughout, marked H

scope.notes: The distinct Medulla grammaticae at ff. 84r-174v is adjacent in the same codex but is not treated as part of the Promptorium witness.

subject.description: Late-stage readings and corrections available to Way after the manuscript's 1858 acquisition
Numerous additions and English/Latin variants marked P
A few readings of minor critical importance marked J

rights.component: The 1843-1865 print volume and its Google-derived scan files

rights.basis: All three parts were published in 1843-1865, and the Google front matter embedded in the downloadable file identifies the digitized work as public domain. The file also requests attribution/noncommercial conduct, which is recorded as a provider term rather than converted into a copyright license.
notes: The assessment does not waive trademarks, privacy interests, database rights, or contractual conditions in other jurisdictions.

rights.component: The two nineteenth-century printed manuscript reproductions as digitized in the Way volume
rights.jurisdiction: United States for the public-domain determination; provider terms may apply internationally
rights.basis: The plates were published as part of the 1843-1865 Way edition and are included in the Google file identified as public domain.
notes: This concerns the historical printed/digitized plates, not hypothetical new photography of the British Library manuscripts.

rights.component: The 1908 print volume and its Google-derived scan files
rights.jurisdiction: United States for the public-domain determination; provider terms may apply internationally
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1908, and the Google front matter embedded in the downloadable file identifies the digitized work as public domain. Any provider requests are treated as terms, not as a copyright license.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Harley MS 221 as base text, with five additional manuscript sources and three early printed versions
editorial_practices.representation: Harley readings normally in the text; additions and variants placed in brackets or apparatus and identified by siglum
editorial_practices.description: Way states that Harley MS 221 is the groundwork of his edition. He marked substantial Pynson material P, manuscript additions K/H/S or shelfmark, and a few Notary/de Worde readings J/W; Add MS 22556 became available only after the earlier parts.
editorial_practices.scope: Way's Promptorium text and apparatus, pp. 1-540 and later additions/corrections

editorial_practices.source_feature: Separate alphabetic sequences for nomina and verba within each letter
editorial_practices.representation: One merged alphabetic sequence for all parts of speech
editorial_practices.description: Way deliberately merged the manuscript/early-print nominale and verbale sequences for reference convenience. The resulting order is editorial and should not be projected back onto a manuscript or the abstract work without qualification.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Gender, declension, conjugation, and Latin inflectional information in manuscript entries
editorial_practices.representation: Much grammatical information omitted, with selected readings and commentary retained
editorial_practices.description: Way says he omitted gender, declension, conjugation, and some Latin inflection where they did not serve his editorial purpose. Mayhew's later Winchester edition preserves more of this source structure.
editorial_practices.scope: Latin equivalents and grammatical apparatus throughout the lexicon

editorial_practices.source_feature: Manuscript and early-print contractions, including ambiguous final m/n marks
editorial_practices.representation: Contractions generally printed in full, with a limited set of uncertain or conventional marks retained
editorial_practices.description: Way explicitly reports expanding contractions in extenso except where uncertainty remained. This intervention sits between the manuscript witnesses and both the U-M text and repository XML.
editorial_practices.scope: Edited Middle English and Latin text

editorial_practices.source_feature: Complete 673-page three-part Way volume with edition text, commentary, frontmatter, appendices, indexes, plates, and series matter
editorial_practices.representation: Two volume titles, preamble, lexicon through p. 540, local notes and early-print colophons, plus 37 addendum entries across the final four encoded pages
editorial_practices.description: The U-M/CME encoding is not a complete digital edition of Way's volume. Structural comparison identifies the included text and the omitted 1865 title, Advertisement, Preface, Appendix, orthographic and subject indexes, plates, and other matter.

evidence.citation: CME00028 XML source in the pinned CME submodule.
evidence.locator: IDG; TEMPHEAD; TITLEPAGE; FRONT/DIV1; BODY/DIV1; BACK/DIV1
evidence.summary: The XML identifies CME00028, U-M's public item, Way's 1843-1865 edition, review history, languages, title divisions, medieval preamble, letter divisions, page references, notes, early-print colophons, and addenda.

evidence.citation: Deterministic XPath and repository inspection of CME00028.xml.
evidence.locator: counts and ranges for DIV1/DIV2/DIV3, LIST/ITEM, NOTE, LG/L, P, PB/@REF, XML bytes/hash, git blob, and pinned submodule commit
evidence.summary: The 2,384,777-byte XML has 23 letter divisions and 11,797 direct lexicon entries (A 422; B 933; C 1,165; D 512; E 210; F 728; G 539; H 530; I 186; K 170; L 536; M 579; N 199; O 311; P 800; Q 76; R 501; S 1,749; T 671; V 176; W 711; thorn 20; yogh 73), plus 37 addendum items. Its three LG and 825 L elements occur inside Way's quoted editorial material, not as intrinsic verse. PB ranges crosswalk the encoded lexicon to Way pp. 5-540 and addenda to the unnumbered opening and pp. 561-563.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, CME00028.
evidence.summary: The current item page identifies Way's 1843-1865 source edition, exposes the encoded contents, and displays a public-domain/No Copyright-United States assertion.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, About the Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse.
evidence.summary: U-M explains that the 2016-2018 expansion manually keyed public-domain editions, proofread and edited the result in-house, and supplied basic TEI encoding. It does not expose an item-specific keying specification for CME00028.

evidence.citation: Albert Way, ed., Promptorium Parvulorum sive Clericorum, Camden Society publications 25, 54, and 89 (London, 1843, 1853, 1865), complete bound three-part scan.
evidence.locator: complete 673-page scan; all three title pages; Advertisement; Preface; pp. 1-563; Appendix; indexes; manuscript plates
evidence.summary: The complete scan establishes the immediate edition's three-part publication, full support matter, CME selection boundary, page-level apparatus, manuscript plates, and Google public-domain notice.

evidence.citation: Albert Way, 'Advertisement,' Promptorium Parvulorum, first part (1843).
evidence.locator: Advertisement, pp. v-vi of the first part
evidence.summary: Way identifies Harley MS 221 as the base, King's MS 8 as K, Phillipps 8306 as H, Winchester as S, Harley 2274 by shelfmark, Pynson 1499 as P, Julian Notary 1508 as J, and de Worde 1516 as W; he also explains bracketing, the merged alphabetic order, and grammatical omissions.

evidence.citation: Albert Way, Preface and manuscript/edition descriptions in the third part of Promptorium Parvulorum (1865).
evidence.locator: Preface, especially pp. xxxvi-xlviii; manuscript descriptions 1-6; early-print descriptions; editorial-method conclusion
evidence.summary: Way gives physical and provenance descriptions of all six manuscript sources, explains the Harley lacuna and Winchester supply, crosswalks his sigla, identifies the Pynson/Notary/de Worde source copies and exact imprints, and states his expansion, collation, correction, omission, and reordering practices.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of the two manuscript plates in the complete Way scan.
evidence.locator: PDF p. 46 / IA n45: 'British Museum. Harl. MS. 221 Fol. 7 verso'; PDF p. 52 / IA n51: 'British Museum. Additional MS. 22,556. Folio 73 verso'
evidence.summary: Direct visual inspection confirms the two labeled black-and-white printed plates and their folio identities. No other page is inferred from them, and neither specimen is treated as a whole-manuscript facsimile.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 221.
evidence.locator: scope, contents, physical characteristics, provenance, related material, access
evidence.summary: The current record identifies the fifteenth-century parchment Promptorium at ff. 1r-205v, adjacent recipes at f. 206r, extent and dimensions, surviving witness census, compiler and source discussion, uncertain Geoffrey attribution, likely non-East-Anglian copying, and absence of a Digitised Content link.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 2274 parent and Promptorium component ff. 63r-77v.
evidence.locator: component scope; contents; parent extent/support and access
evidence.summary: The current records identify a disordered Promptorium fragment on ff. 63r-77v containing G, H, M, N, O, and R within a 77-folio composite from the second half of the fifteenth century and expose no Digitised Content.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Add MS 37789.
evidence.locator: Promptorium ff. 1r-83v; Medulla ff. 84r-174v; physical characteristics; provenance; access
evidence.summary: The current record crosswalks Way's H and former Phillipps 8306/Heber 1360 to Add MS 37789, distinguishes its imperfect Promptorium from the adjacent Medulla grammaticae, localizes the late-fifteenth-century paper codex to Norfolk, and exposes no Digitised Content.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Add MS 22556.
evidence.locator: scope; contents; physical characteristics; provenance; access
evidence.summary: The current record identifies the imperfect fifteenth-century parchment witness, modern Way letter and notes at ff. 1-3, surviving Promptorium material at ff. 5r-110v, current witness census, and absence of a Digitised Content link.

evidence.citation: King's College Cambridge, Library.
evidence.locator: library, special-collections, and research contact routes
evidence.summary: Current institutional access route for rare books and manuscripts. No exact public item page or complete digital object for MS 8 was located; its shelfmark and description were therefore cross-checked against Way, Mayhew, Considine, and the current BL witness census.

evidence.citation: Winchester Cathedral, Library and Archives.
evidence.locator: manuscript collection, catalog state, and curator enquiry information
evidence.summary: The Cathedral states that manuscript records remain chiefly printed or handwritten and provides a curator enquiry route. It exposes no item-level digital record or whole-object facsimile for MS 15.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation Services.
evidence.locator: online ordering; required manuscript shelfmark/folio information; original-manuscript photography; restrictions and licensing contact
evidence.summary: The current service accepts paid requests for images from BL manuscripts, requires shelfmarks and folio sides, may use expert photography for rare originals, cannot scan original manuscripts, and subjects fulfillment and reuse to feasibility and permissions. No order was placed.

evidence.citation: British Library, Imaging Services terms and conditions, updated January 2026.
evidence.locator: preservation, turnaround, pricing, and copyright/reproduction terms
evidence.summary: The terms make requests subject to retrieval, condition, handling, fees, and individual discussion and state that delivered material may have intellectual-property restrictions and may not be copied or reproduced without written permission.

evidence.citation: Penn Libraries Colenda, Inc P-1011 Kislak copy, Promptorius paruulorum siue clericorum (Richard Pynson, 1499).
evidence.locator: catalog description; leaves a1v-t3v; colophon; signatures; physical copy; provenance; rights; IIIF canvases 1-238
evidence.summary: The current institutional record and complete facsimile establish the 15 May 1499 imprint, variant titles, Latin prologue, Middle English-Latin list, colophon, signatures, 116-leaf ideal extent, the Kislak copy's missing blank t4, copy-specific binding/provenance/condition, and NoC-US assertion.

evidence.citation: Inspection and complete caching of Penn Libraries IIIF Presentation manifest ark:/81431/p32n4zz38.
evidence.locator: 238 canvases; canvas 8/a1v prologue; canvas 9/a2r lexicon incipit; canvas 231/t3r colophon; canvas 232/t3v device; canvases 1-7 and 233-238 binding/endmatter
evidence.summary: The cached ZIP preserves the complete 238-canvas exposed physical copy, source manifest, normalized canvas/image inventory, and exact work boundaries. Inventory checks confirm one image per canvas, 238 source files, and non-upscaled 1600-pixel-wide image requests.

evidence.citation: Gesamtkatalog der Wiegendrucke, GW 10483, Promptorium parvulorum.
evidence.locator: imprint, collation, incipit, colophon, census, and reproductions
evidence.summary: The incunable authority independently confirms Pynson's London printing for Frederick Egmondt and Petrus post Pascha on 15 May 1499, the 116-leaf ideal collation, textual incipits/colophon, surviving copies, and reproduction references.

evidence.citation: Laurent Brun, 'Promptorium parvulorum,' ARLIMA: Archives de littérature du Moyen Âge, updated 23 June 2026.
evidence.locator: manuscripts, early editions, modern editions, and online-copy links
evidence.summary: The current scholarly discovery record cross-references Harley 221 and Winchester, the 1499 Pynson and 1508 Notary editions, GW/ISTC/USTC identifiers, the Penn and BL online copies, and the complete Way and Mayhew editions. Its generic manuscript viewer links were not assumed to prove digitization without an exposed manifest/canvas.

evidence.citation: A. L. Mayhew, ed., The Promptorium Parvulorum: The First English-Latin Dictionary, EETS Extra Series 102 (London, 1908), complete scan.
evidence.locator: complete 591-page scan; title; Introduction pp. vii-xxviii; Winchester text; notes and glossaries
evidence.summary: The later comparison edition directly presents Winchester Cathedral MS 15, reviews Way's sources and method, preserves the original nomina/verba arrangement and more grammatical information, and confirms that it is not Way's source or the source of CME00028.

evidence.citation: John Considine, Sixteenth-Century English Dictionaries (Oxford: Oxford University Press, 2022), chapter 1, pp. 20-25.
evidence.locator: chapter 1, 'The Catholicon Anglicum and the Promptorium parvulorum,' especially pp. 20-25
evidence.summary: Recent scholarship reconstructs an accreting Promptorium tradition derived largely from the Medulla tradition, distinguishes original work from expanded Harley/Winchester redactions, identifies nine extant whole or fragmentary fifteenth-century witnesses, rejects the Geoffrey attribution as Bale-derived, and treats Hearne's reported Richard Fraunces attribution as plausible but unverified.
notes: The copyrighted preview was consulted online but not cached or represented as an open support volume.

open_questions.question: Can complete, reusable whole-object digital facsimiles be obtained for all six manuscript sources used by Way?
next_steps: Ask the British Library to assess complete-object photography of Harley MS 221, Harley MS 2274, Add MS 37789, and Add MS 22556, including covers, bindings, endleaves, blanks, adjacent texts, and every folio rather than only the Promptorium portions.

Ask King's College Library and Winchester Cathedral Library to confirm current shelfmarks/catalog records, image availability, whole-codex reproduction feasibility, fees, and reuse terms for MS 8 and MS 15.
Record delivered-image rights and contract terms before caching any requested files.
notes: The two cached Way plates are expressly partial and do not satisfy this question.
Generic IIIF/Mirador viewer chrome without a manifest or canvases is not evidence that a manuscript has been digitized.

open_questions.question: Does the 1440 date record the original compilation or only the expanded redaction represented by Harley MS 221, and how are the nine extant witnesses related stemmatically?
next_steps: Compare the preambles, additions, omissions, source lists, and nomina/verba sequences across all extant whole and fragmentary witnesses rather than treating Way's collated text as the original work.
Test Considine's model that Add MS 37789 represents an earlier stage and Harley/Winchester later accretions against complete witness images and diplomatic transcriptions.
Integrate the Emmanuel College pastedown fragments and other witnesses not used by Way into a tradition-level census.

open_questions.question: What item-specific U-M production record identifies the exact source copy, transcription/keying vendor or staff workflow, and selection rationale for CME00028?
next_steps: Ask U-M Library for the item-level keying specification, source-copy identifier, transformation history, proofreading log, and rationale for the encoded Way boundaries.
Compare any supplied production record with TEMPHEAD and the deterministic XML-to-print boundary analysis.

open_questions.question: What are the current shelfmarks and complete digital-surrogate states of the exact Julian Notary 1508 and Wynkyn de Worde 1516 copies collated by Way?
next_steps: Crosswalk Way's Grenville 1508 copy and former Heber/Madden 1516 copy through current British Library and other institutional provenance records.
Recheck ESTC, USTC, EEBO, institutional catalogs, and newly exposed IIIF services for complete copy-specific surrogates, distinguishing subscription access from reusable public images.
If a public copy is found, cache the complete physical volume including binding and adjacent works and record exact Way-work boundaries.

review_notes: The abstract Promptorium is cataloged in the paired work-metadata record; the lineage primary subject is the concrete repository artifact, following the repository model.
Way's H is crosswalked to current Add MS 37789 / former Phillipps 8306 and Heber 1360. The adjacent Medulla grammaticae is explicitly excluded from the Promptorium witness scope.
The three LG and 825 L nodes in the XML are quotations inside Way's editorial apparatus, so they do not change the medieval work's prose form.
No external reproduction request was sent. All whole-manuscript negatives are dated research results, not claims that no private, subscription, or future surrogate exists.
